meaning: n.[蛹ｻ]蜉ｨ閼臥ｲ･譬ｷ遑ｬ蛹匁ぅ閠Ëdj.蜉ｨ閼臥ｲ･譬ｷ遑ｬ蛹也噪
examples: Calcium tends to build up in atherosclerotic plaques.
Carotid atherosclerotic plaque in the carotid artery made good forks.
Objective To explore the risk factors of carotid artery atherosclerotic plaque in ischemic stroke patients.
That's because the arteries get clogged with clumps of fat, cholesterol and other material, called atherosclerotic plaques.
That is the 'bad' cholesterol which forms atherosclerotic lesions, the fatty deposits which narrow the arteries and lead to heart disease.
